description: Expand the underfilled README Research Papers (7→≥15) and Blog Posts (6→≥10) sections with academically relevant papers and substantive technical articles that preserve scope discipline, alphabetization, canonical URLs, neutral descriptions, and agent-factory relevance keywords; pass make check, make test, and git diff --check.

context.customerAsk: Phase 7 content expansion: after the core section update lands, expand the README Research Papers and Blog Posts sections to meet the customer ask minimums while preserving quality. Increase Research Papers from 7 entries to at least 15 and Blog Posts from 6 entries to at least 10. Use academically relevant papers and technical articles only; avoid launch posts, shallow trend pieces, generic AI content, and vendor marketing. Keep sections alphabetized, descriptions neutral, and every added item clearly justified by the agent-factory angle.
context.problem: Research Papers and Blog Posts remain below Phase 7 minimum targets. Readers lack enough on-main academic references and practitioner writeups to study multi-agent coordination, learn from production lessons, and compare acceptable technical depth against borderline submissions. Thin writing sections weaken maintainer scope enforcement.
context.solution: Add curated entries only to the two target README sections until each meets the minimum count bar, following docs/taxonomy.md include rules and CONTRIBUTING.md formatting and relevance rules. Preserve governance prose and all non-target curated sections. Verify make check, make test, and git diff --check pass before merge.

acceptanceCriteria: README Research Papers contains at least 15 resource entries (currently 7) using - [Resource Name](URL) - Description. format with descriptions ending in a period and entries alphabetized by link text
README Blog Posts contains at least 10 resource entries (currently 6) using the same format, tone, and alphabetization rules

Every new entry is academically relevant (papers) or a substantive technical article (blogs); excludes launch posts, shallow trend pieces, generic AI content, and vendor marketing per docs/taxonomy.md and docs/rejected.md
Every new entry includes an agent-factory scope keyword in the description and introduces no duplicate URLs anywhere in README.md
README governance sections (Scope, Contributing, Community) and all non-target curated sections remain unchanged except unavoidable whitespace or merge hygiene
Quality gate: make check, make test, and git diff --check all pass from the repository root

userStories.title: Expand Research Papers section to minimum density
userStories.description: As a researcher or architect studying multi-agent systems, I want more academically relevant papers indexed on main so I can explore coordination, communication, planning, societies, evaluation, and governance research beyond the current seven seed entries.
acceptanceCriteria: README Research Papers contains at least 15 entries below the section intro (at least 8 new entries beyond the existing seven seed entries)

Each new entry is peer-reviewed or widely cited research on multi-agent coordination, orchestration, communication, planning, delegation, agent societies, group-level evaluation, safety, or governance per docs/taxonomy.md Research Papers include rules—not a blog post, vendor whitepaper, framework repository, or benchmark landing page
New entries diversify themes beyond the current survey-and-framework-paper cluster without duplicating resources whose primary home is another README section
Each entry uses exact format - [Resource Name](URL) - Description. with a factual one-sentence description ending in a period and an explicit agent-factory scope keyword
Entries are alphabetized by link text across the full section and no duplicate URLs are introduced anywhere in README.md
make check passes after Research Papers expansion
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Expand Blog Posts section to minimum density
userStories.description: As a practitioner building agent factories, I want more technical articles indexed on main so I can learn from architecture breakdowns, failure analyses, and production orchestration lessons beyond the current six seed entries.
acceptanceCriteria: README Blog Posts contains at least 10 entries below the section intro (at least 4 new entries beyond the existing six seed entries)

Each new entry is technical writing that teaches how groups of agents were designed, operated, or measured per docs/taxonomy.md Blog Posts include rules—not a launch announcement, shallow trend piece, generic AI overview, prompt collection, or primarily promotional vendor page
New URLs do not duplicate URLs already present elsewhere in README.md including Coordination Patterns and other curated sections
Each entry uses exact format - [Resource Name](URL) - Description. with a factual one-sentence description ending in a period and an explicit agent-factory scope keyword
Entries are alphabetized by link text across the full section
make check passes after Blog Posts expansion
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Verify batch quality gates and section integrity
userStories.description: As a maintainer merging this batch, I want end-to-end verification that expanded research and writing sections satisfy automated checks and leave unrelated repository content untouched.
acceptanceCriteria: From repository root, make check exits 0
From repository root, make test exits 0
git diff --check reports no whitespace errors on changed files
README Research Papers contains at least 15 entries and Blog Posts contains at least 10 entries with at least 12 combined new entries relative to pre-batch counts
README Scope, Contributing, and Community sections remain present and unweakened; non-target curated sections contain no unintended edits
Changed content files are limited to README.md and planning artifacts for this batch
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
